---
title: "Digital Identity for Researchers"
subtitle: "Nicolás Robinson-García · University of Granada"
---

![](images/digital-identity.png){fig-align="center" width="409"}

This workshop covers the essentials of managing your online presence as a researcher. We'll go from the basics (making sure your publications are correctly attributed to you) to the more strategic (deciding *when* and *how* to engage publicly with your work).

## Structure

| Part | Topic                                |
|------|--------------------------------------|
| 1    | Introduction: why visibility matters |
| 2    | CV and researcher identifiers        |
| 3    | Digital identity and open access     |

## A note on priorities

Not everything here applies equally to everyone nor work for every profile. Many of the tips I provide are either based on my one experience or that of colleagues from my field and other fields.

## Materials

Slides and links referenced during the session are embedded in each section. All materials are openly available upon attribution.
